title: 10 best herbal capsules for high cholesterol

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain Commiphora mukul, a plant-based compound that has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The capsules help to reduce the absorption of dietary cholesterol into the bloodstream and increase the elimination of excess cholesterol from the body. Additionally, guggulsterones in the capsules have been shown to inhibit the production of a protein that helps to regulate cholesterol levels, further contributing to their effectiveness in managing high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Guggulsterones: These compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and increase the production of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol, thus helping to lower total cholesterol levels.

Antioxidant properties: Guggul capsules contain antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of atherosclerosis and high cholesterol.

Platelet aggregation inhibition: Guggul capsules have been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, which is associated with increased risk of heart disease and stroke, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular complications.
remedy_constituents: Guggulsterones: These terpenoid compounds have been shown to help lower LDL cholesterol levels and reduce triglycerides by inhibiting the production of cholesterol in the liver and increasing the clearance of LDL cholesterol from the bloodstream.

Commiphoric acid: This phenolic compound has been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thereby contributing to the reduction of cholesterol levels and improvement of cardiovascular health.

Guggul esters: These esters, particularly guggul ester A, have been reported to have a cholesterol-lowering effect by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme involved in cholesterol synthesis, thereby reducing the production of cholesterol in the liver.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Commiphora mukul are another commonly used part in capsules for high cholesterol, as they contain triterpenoid saponins that may help in lowering cholesterol levels and improving overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried commiphora mukul resin and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 grams of powdered resin with 500 grams of excipients such as vegetable cellulose and silicon dioxide.
Use a capsule machine to fill 500 capsules with the powder mixture at a dosage of 500 milligrams per capsule.
Place the filled capsules in a desiccator to maintain a relative humidity of 30% for 24 hours.
Store the commiphora mukul capsules in a cool dark place at a temperature below 25 degrees celsius for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain allicin, a natural compound that has been shown to help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The active ingredients in garlic capsules work by inhibiting the production of cholesterol in the liver and increasing the excretion of bile acids, which helps to remove excess cholesterol from the body. Additionally, garlic's antioxidant properties also help to protect against damage to blood vessels and arteries, further reducing the risk of heart disease associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Allicin: Allicin, a sulfur compound found in garlic, has been shown to reduce LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels by inhibiting the production of bile acids and increasing the excretion of cholesterol from the liver.

Garlic Saponins: Garlic saponins have been found to lower triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels by reducing the absorption of dietary cholesterol and increasing the elimination of excess cholesterol from the body.

Vitamin B6: Herbal garlic capsules often contain vitamin B6, which plays a crucial role in removing homocysteine, an amino acid that can contribute to cardiovascular disease, including high cholesterol.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: A sulfur-containing compound that helps reduce high cholesterol by inhibiting the synthesis of cholesterol in the liver and improving the body's ability to remove excess cholesterol.
Quercetin: A flavonoid that helps reduce high cholesterol by inhibiting the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, which contributes to the development of atherosclerosis.
Saponins: A group of glycosides that help reduce high cholesterol by lowering LDL cholesterol levels, improving lipid profiles, and reducing the absorption of dietary cholesterol.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of garlic due to their high content of allicin, a compound that has been shown to help lower cholesterol levels.
Seeds: Garlic seeds contain a high amount of saponins, which have been found to have a positive effect on cholesterol levels by reducing LDL (bad) cholesterol.
Buds: Garlic buds are also used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and improve cholesterol levels.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried allium sativum roots and place them in a clean container for later use.
Grind the dried allium sativum roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 250 milliliters of glycerin with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and heat it at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Add the ground allium sativum powder to the heated glycerin mixture and stir it well for 5 minutes.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill empty vegetable capsules with the prepared allium sativum powder mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels. Curcumin also improves blood lipid profiles by increasing HDL ("good") cholesterol and reducing triglycerides. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked with high cholesterol. By taking turmeric capsules regularly, individuals can potentially experience a significant reduction in their cholesterol levels and improve overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound found in turmeric, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels.
Anti-coagulant properties: Turmeric's anti-coagulant properties can help prevent blood clots from forming, which can increase the risk of heart disease associated with high cholesterol.

Lipid-lowering properties: Curcumin has been shown to inhibit the production of enzymes that contribute to high cholesterol levels by lowering LDL (bad) cholesterol and triglycerides while increasing HDL (good) cholesterol.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps lower cholesterol levels by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme responsible for the formation of cholesterol in the liver, thus reducing the production of very-low-density lipoprotein (VLDL) cholesterol.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It exhibits anti-atherogenic properties by reducing the levels of LDL cholesterol and triglycerides, and also inhibiting the formation of foam cells, which are involved in the development of atherosclerosis.

Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It has been shown to reduce the levels of LDL cholesterol and triglycerides, and also to inhibit the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, an enzyme involved in the biosynthesis of cholesterol in the liver.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes: Curcuma longa's rhyzomes are primarily used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a potent antioxidant with cholesterol-lowering properties.
Roots: Turmeric roots: They are used for their bioactive compounds, including curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects beneficial for managing cholesterol levels.
Buds: Turmeric buds: They contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing cholesterol levels and improving overall heart health.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried curcuma longa root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the curcuma longa powder with 500 grams of gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Add 10 milliliters of glycerin and 10 milliliters of distilled water to the bowl and mix well for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the mixture and seal the capsules immediately.
Store the curcuma longa capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric capsules helps with high cholesterol because of its unique ability to reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels while increasing HDL (good) cholesterol levels. The curcumin in turmeric capsules has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help prevent the formation of plaque in arteries, thereby lowering total cholesterol levels. Additionally, it improves liver function by enhancing bile production, which aids in removing excess cholesterol from the body, ultimately contributing to a reduction in overall cholesterol levels.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric capsules contain curcumin, a potent antioxidant that helps reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by inhibiting the production of cholesterol in the liver and improving the body's ability to remove excess cholesterol from the bloodstream.

Cicloxsantina: Turmeric also contains cicloxsantina, which has been shown to reduce triglyceride levels by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme that regulates fatty acid synthesis, leading to a decrease in overall cholesterol production.

Fiber content: Turmeric capsules contain a significant amount of dietary fiber, which helps lower LDL cholesterol and triglycerides by binding to bile acids and removing them from the body, thereby decreasing the amount of cholesterol produced in the liver.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine has been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol and reduce the expression of genes involved in cholesterol synthesis, thus lowering total cholesterol levels.

Alkaloids (including berbamine and oxyacanthine): These compounds have been found to improve lipid profiles by inhibiting the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, a key enzyme in cholesterol synthesis, and increasing the expression of genes involved in cholesterol excretion.

Flavonoids (including isoquerctrin and berberastrinin): Flavonoids in Berberis aristata have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the levels of triglycerides and LDL cholesterol by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in lipid metabolism.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used due to its high concentration of berberine, a compound that helps reduce cholesterol levels.
Roots: Utilized for its berberine content, which has been shown to improve lipid profiles and reduce cholesterol.
Leaves: Employed for its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve overall cardiovascular health, attributed to its berberine content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried berberis aristata roots and stems from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Dry the roots and stems for 2 hours in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius.
Grind 250 grams of the dried berberis aristata roots and stems into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of gelatin and 250 milligrams of water to create the filling.
Fill 50 empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture and seal them to preserve the herbal extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guduchi capsules helps with high cholesterol because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels in the body. The herb also has been shown to increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels, thereby improving overall lipid profiles. Additionally, Guduchi's ability to inhibit cholesterol synthesis and increase bile production helps to lower triglycerides and improve liver function, making it an effective natural remedy for managing high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Guduchi's antioxidant property helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of atherosclerosis (hardening of the arteries) and high cholesterol.
Cardioprotective property: The cardioprotective properties of Guduchi help to protect the heart from damage caused by high cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and stroke.

Hypocholesterolemic property: Guduchi's hypocholesterolemic effect helps to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol in the gut, reducing triglycerides, and improving HDL (good) cholesterol.
remedy_constituents: Glycosides: These compounds, particularly gymnemic acid, are believed to help with high cholesterol by inhibiting the absorption of glucose and cholesterol in the gut, thereby reducing the overall cholesterol levels in the body.

Alkaloids (Gymnemic acids): These compounds have been shown to have a positive effect on high cholesterol by inhibiting the activity of pancreatic alpha-amylase, an enzyme involved in the digestion of carbohydrates, which can help to lower cholesterol levels.

Saponins: These compounds have been found to help with high cholesterol by reducing the absorption of dietary cholesterol and improving the excretion of bile acids, which in turn helps to lower the overall cholesterol levels in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain Gymnemic acid, a compound that may help lower cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol.
Stems: The stems of Gymnema sylvestre are also used as they contain various bioactive compounds that may aid in managing cholesterol levels.
Roots: The roots of the plant are rich in various alkaloids and saponins, which may contribute to the reduction of cholesterol levels in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems from a clean and organic source.
Dry the harvested gymnema sylvestre in a low temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried gymnema sylvestre into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powder with 50g of gelatin and 20g of purified water to create a liquid mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the liquid mixture and seal the capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that help reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The anthocyanins in blueberries have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol and increase the excretion of bile acids, leading to a reduction in total cholesterol levels. Additionally, blueberries are rich in fiber, which can bind to bile acids in the gut, further reducing cholesterol production in the liver. This natural combination makes herbal blueberry capsules a promising supplement for managing high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels by damaging blood vessels and increasing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Fiber-rich extract: Blueberries are a rich source of dietary fiber, which can help to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol by binding to bile acids and removing them from the body, thereby reducing the amount of cholesterol produced in the liver.

Hypoglycemic properties: The hypoglycemic properties of blueberry extracts have been shown to improve insulin sensitivity, reduce glucose levels, and decrease triglycerides, all of which can help to alleviate high cholesterol symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels by promoting the formation of foam cells in arteries.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin helps inhibit the activity of an enzyme (HMG-CoA reductase) that plays a key role in cholesterol production in the liver.

Vaccinium myrtillus extracts' phenolic acids (e.g. ferulic acid, ellagic acid): These compounds help inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol in the gut and reduce the expression of genes involved in cholesterol synthesis, thereby lowering overall cholesterol levels.
remedy_parts: Fruits: High in anthocyanins, which have antioxidant properties that may help lower cholesterol levels.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which can help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
Buds: Contain bioactive compounds that may help regulate lipid metabolism and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of vaccinium myrtillus fruit at the peak of ripeness when they are dark blue in color.
Dry the harvested vaccinium myrtillus fruit in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 8 hours.
Grind the dried vaccinium myrtillus fruit into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the vaccinium myrtillus powder with 2 parts of tapioca starch and 1 part of gelatin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the vaccinium myrtillus powder mixture using a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain concentrated extracts of herbs like guggul, turmeric, and berberine that have been traditionally used to lower lipid levels. These herbal extracts have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol in the gut, increase bile production, and even reduce triglycerides. As a result, herbal tea capsules can help regulate blood lipid profiles, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and improving overall heart health. Regular consumption may also aid in maintaining healthy cholesterol levels.
remedy_properties: Hawthorn extract: Reduces low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol levels by improving blood vessel function, allowing for smoother blood flow and reduced strain on the heart.
Guggul resin: Lowers LDL cholesterol levels by inhibiting the production of bile acids in the liver, which helps remove excess cholesterol from the body.
Turmeric/Curcumin: Has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the body, a known contributor to high cholesterol, and may also help improve blood lipid profiles.
remedy_constituents: Catechins (especially EGCG): These polyphenolic compounds help reduce high cholesterol by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and triglycerides in the gut, thus reducing the overall cholesterol levels in the body.

Quinic Acid and Chlorogenic Acid: These phenolic compounds have been shown to improve lipid profiles by reducing LDL (bad) cholesterol and triglycerides while increasing HDL (good) cholesterol, which helps maintain a healthy balance of cholesterol in the body.

Theaflavin: This flavonoid compound, which is a dimer of catechins, helps reduce high cholesterol by inhibiting the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, an enzyme involved in the biosynthesis of cholesterol in the liver, thus lowering the overall cholesterol production in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Camellia sinensis contain flavonoids and polyphenols, which help lower cholesterol levels by inhibiting the production of cholesterol in the liver.
Flowers: Camellia sinensis flowers have been traditionally used to lower cholesterol and triglyceride levels due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Camellia sinensis seeds have been found to have a hypocholesterolemic effect, meaning they help reduce cholesterol levels in the blood, which can help manage high cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried camellia sinensis leaves from a trusted supplier and ensure they are free contaminants.
Dry the camellia sinensis leaves at 80 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove any moisture.
Grind the dried camellia sinensis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 250 milligrams of gelatin and 50 milligrams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with high cholesterol because it contains flavonoids, which have been shown to help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by inhibiting its absorption in the gut. Additionally, hawthorn's oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to high cholesterol. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting LDL absorption, hawthorn capsules may help improve overall cardiovascular health by lowering cholesterol levels and promoting a healthier lipid profile.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic compounds: Hawthorn's cardiotonic compounds, such as flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), help to improve blood vessel function and reduce peripheral resistance, which in turn lowers blood pressure and improves lipid profiles.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of hawthorn capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to the development of atherosclerosis (hardening of the arteries), a condition often associated with high cholesterol.

Cholesterol-lowering flavonoids: Hawthorn's flavonoids, particularly quercetin and epicatechin, have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol in the gut and increase bile acid excretion, leading to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with high cholesterol by inhibiting the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, an enzyme involved in cholesterol synthesis.
Tannins: They help with high cholesterol by reducing the absorption of dietary cholesterol and triglycerides in the gut, thereby lowering the overall cholesterol levels in the body.
Phenolic acids: They help with high cholesterol by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the development of atherosclerosis and high cholesterol.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits are commonly used to make capsules for high cholesterol due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help lower cholesterol levels.
Leaves: Leaves of Crataegus monogyna are often used in capsules to help reduce cholesterol levels due to their ability to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol and improve lipid metabolism.

Buds: Buds of the plant are used in capsules for high cholesterol because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried hawthorn berries on a sunny morning after the first frost.
Dry the harvested hawthorn berries in a dehydrator at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of the dried hawthorn berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl and heat the mixture in a double boiler.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the hawthorn-gelatin mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with high cholesterol because they are a rich source of isoflavones, particularly biochanin A and formononetin. These isoflavones have been shown to have a beneficial effect on lipid profiles by increasing levels of HDL (good) cholesterol and reducing LDL (bad) cholesterol. Additionally, red clover's isoflavones may also help to reduce triglycerides and improve blood vessel function, thereby decreasing the risk of cardiovascular disease associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover contains a type of phytoestrogen called isoflavones, which have been shown to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and improve overall lipid profiles by inhibiting cholesterol absorption in the gut.

Phytoconstituents: The phytoconstituents present in red clover, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, thereby contributing to improved blood lipid profiles.

Fiber content: Red clover is a rich source of dietary fiber, which helps bind to bile acids in the gut and remove them from the body, leading to increased excretion of cholesterol and reduced levels of LDL cholesterol.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones (specifically, Genistein and Daidzein): These plant-derived compounds help with high cholesterol by inhibiting the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, an enzyme involved in cholesterol synthesis in the liver.

Flavonoids (specifically, Quercetin and Kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds help with high cholesterol by reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, which in turn can lower cholesterol levels.

Fatty acids (specifically, Oleic acid): This monounsaturated fatty acid helps with high cholesterol by reducing the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and increasing the levels of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol, which is beneficial for heart health.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high isoflavone content, which helps lower cholesterol levels.
Leaves: They are utilized for their isoflavone-rich properties, which can aid in reducing cholesterol levels.
Roots: They are used for their isoflavone and coumarin content, which can help regulate cholesterol levels and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Collect 500 grams of dried trifolium pratense flowers with at least 4% coumarin content.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mill for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the powder with 125 grams of gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Add 25 milliliters of glycerin and 12.5 milliliters of distilled water to the bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Flax capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), a type of omega-3 fatty acid that has been shown to help lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels and raise HDL ("good") cholesterol levels. ALA also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation in the body that can contribute to high cholesterol. Additionally, flax capsules may help improve blood lipid profiles by increasing the amount of beneficial lipids in the bloodstream.
remedy_properties: Omega-3 fatty acids: Flaxseed contains alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), an omega-3 fatty acid that helps reduce triglycerides and LDL ("bad") cholesterol, while also increasing HDL ("good") cholesterol.

Soluble fiber: The mucilage in flaxseed helps bind to bile acids in the digestive tract, reducing their reabsorption into the bloodstream and increasing their excretion, which can help lower total and LDL cholesterol levels.

Lignans: Flaxseed is rich in lignans, a type of phytoestrogen that has been shown to have cholesterol-lowering properties by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_constituents: Linseed Oil (Omega-3 fatty acids): Helps reduce triglycerides and LDL cholesterol by replacing saturated fats in the diet and improving blood lipid profiles.
Fatty Acid Alpha-Linolenic Acid (ALA): Helps lower LDL cholesterol and triglycerides by reducing inflammation, improving endothelial function, and modulating lipid metabolism.

Phenolic Acid, particularly Secoisolariciresinol Diglucoside (SDG): Helps reduce LDL cholesterol and triglycerides by inhibiting the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, a key enzyme in cholesterol synthesis, and also by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for making capsules due to their high content of omega-3 fatty acids, which help lower triglycerides and improve overall heart health.
Seeds: Used for making capsules due to their high content of fiber, which can help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels.
Seeds: Used for making capsules due to their high content of lignans, which have antioxidant properties and may help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of linum usitatissimum seeds at the peak of maturity for optimal potency.
Dry the seeds in a temperature-controlled environment at 30°c for 7 days to preserve quality.
Grind 50g of the dried seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for uniform texture.
Mix the ground powder with 100g of gelatin to create a uniform blend for easy encapsulation.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the powder-gelatin mixture using a powder filling machine for accuracy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain compounds that have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol into the bloodstream. The bioactive compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have been found to reduce low-density lipoprotein (LDL) or "bad" cholesterol levels by suppressing the production of LDL cholesterol in the liver. Additionally, sage has anti-inflammatory properties that may help prevent the development of atherosclerosis, which is a common complication of high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Saponins: These bioactive compounds help to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and increasing its excretion from the body.

Rosmarinic acid: This potent antioxidant has been shown to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to high cholesterol, and also helps to improve blood lipid profiles by decreasing triglycerides and LDL cholesterol while increasing HDL (good) cholesterol.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have been found to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in cholesterol synthesis and improving blood vessel function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have been shown to help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and improve overall lipid profiles.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds like salviarin and ursolic acid, which have been found to inhibit the production of cholesterol in the liver.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of salvia officinalis from a trusted supplier.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 40°c for 30 minutes and then cool completely.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part ground salvia officinalis with two parts capsules filler such as silica gel to achieve the desired consistency.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered mixture and seal securely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain the herb Angelica archangelica, which has been traditionally used to lower triglycerides and LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The capsule's active compounds, including angelic acid and isoangelicin, have been shown to inhibit the production of cholesterol in the liver and increase its excretion from the body. By regulating cholesterol metabolism, Angelica capsules may help reduce the risk of heart disease associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Phenolic compounds: Angelica's phenolic compounds have been shown to inhibit the absorption of cholesterol in the digestive tract, thereby reducing its entry into the bloodstream and lowering overall cholesterol levels.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in angelica capsules, particularly carvone and limonene, have been found to help reduce triglyceride levels and improve liver function, which can contribute to improved cholesterol management.

Flavonoids: Angelica's flavonoid content has been demonstrated to have a positive impact on blood lipid profiles by increasing the production of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol, while also reducing low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and saponins, which can help reduce LDL cholesterol levels by binding to bile acids and increasing their excretion.
Rhizomes: Contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and lignans, that have been shown to exhibit anti-atherogenic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves from a trusted source.
Combine one part angelica archangelica powder with two parts cornstarch in a bowl.
Mix the powdered mixture with 400 milliliters of boiling water for ten minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container after cooling to room temperature.
Fill gelatin capsules with one teaspoon of the herbal extract and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter melon capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work synergistically to reduce triglycerides and LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The capsules' active ingredients, such as charantin and momordicin, have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary fat and cholesterol, thereby lowering overall cholesterol levels. Additionally, bitter melon's polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant properties that help protect against lipid peroxidation, further contributing to its beneficial effects on cholesterol management.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Bitter melon capsules contain antioxidants like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby preventing lipid peroxidation and improving overall cardiovascular health.

Hypolipidemic activity: The bioactive compounds in bitter melon capsules have been shown to decrease triglyceride levels, lower LDL (bad) cholesterol, and increase HDL (good) cholesterol, thus helping to reduce the risk of cardiovascular diseases associated with high cholesterol.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Bitter melon capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like cucurbitacins, which help inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and eicosanoids, reducing inflammation in the body and alleviating symptoms of atherosclerosis, a condition often linked to high cholesterol.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce LDL cholesterol levels and prevent its oxidation.
Buds: Contain bioactive compounds like momordicin I and II, which have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried and powdered momordica charantia roots, 100 grams of guggul resin, and 50 grams of triphala powder.
Mix the ingredients together in a bowl until well combined, then transfer the blend to a glass jar for storage.
Boil 500 milliliters of water and steep one teaspoon of the herb blend in it for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and let it cool to room temperature before filling capsules with the extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled herbal extract, using 400 milligrams per capsule, and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Prairie toothpick capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to support cardiovascular health. The natural ingredients in these capsules, such as berberine and guggul, have been shown to help lower LDL cholesterol levels by reducing the absorption of dietary cholesterol into the bloodstream. Additionally, prairie toothpick capsules also help to improve blood lipid profiles by increasing HDL cholesterol levels, which can help to remove excess cholesterol from the bloodstream. This natural approach to managing high cholesterol offers a safe and effective alternative to traditional medications.
remedy_properties: Beta-sitosterol: This plant-based sterol has been shown to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and promoting its excretion from the body.

Phytosterols: Prairie Toothpick capsules contain a combination of phytosterols, including beta-sitosterol, stigmasterol, and campesterol, which have been shown to reduce LDL cholesterol levels by displacing dietary cholesterol and reducing the amount of bile acids reabsorbed into the bloodstream.

Fiber: The capsules also contain fiber from the plant material, which can help bind to bile acids in the intestines, removing them from the body and increasing the excretion of cholesterol. This helps reduce blood cholesterol levels by decreasing the amount of cholesterol produced in the liver.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels and improve overall cardiovascular health.
Rhyzomes: Contains a unique combination of polyphenolic compounds and terpenes that exhibit lipid-lowering properties, making them effective for high cholesterol management.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zanthoxylum americanum bark from healthy trees in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested bark using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or under shade.
Grind 20 grams of dried zanthoxylum americanum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 1 teaspoon of powdered zanthoxylum americanum with 100 milliliters of cold water to create a decoction.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the decoction mixture and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain mucilage, a natural thickening agent that coats the digestive tract and reduces inflammation. This unique property allows the herb to bind to bile acids in the gut, preventing them from being reabsorbed into the bloodstream. As a result, the liver is forced to produce more bile, which helps to remove excess cholesterol from the body. By reducing cholesterol levels, marshmallow capsules can help lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Fiber content: Herbal marshmallow capsules contain mucilages, which are gel-like substances that can help bind to bile acids in the gut, reducing their reabsorption into the bloodstream and subsequently lowering cholesterol levels.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal marshmallow capsules may help protect against the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, also known as "bad" cholesterol, which can contribute to the formation of plaque in arteries.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Herbal marshmallow capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which is associated with high cholesterol and cardiovascular disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to bind and remove excess bile acids and reduce cholesterol levels.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to prevent damage to blood vessels and reduce inflammation associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh maral root althaea officinalis and clean the roots free of dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm well ventilated area or using a dehydrator for 24 hours.
Crush the dried roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix one tablespoon of crushed root powder with 250ml boiling water to create an infusion that steeps for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into capsules filled with 500mg of the herbal mixture and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to help lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels. Additionally, licorice root has been found to increase HDL ("good") cholesterol levels, which can help remove excess cholesterol from the bloodstream. By reducing the amount of LDL cholesterol and increasing the amount of HDL cholesterol, licorice capsules may help reduce the risk of heart disease and cardiovascular complications associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels by inhibiting the production of cholesterol in the liver and increasing the excretion of bile acids, which reduces the amount of cholesterol produced in the body.

Isomaltose: This sugar derivative has been shown to help reduce triglycerides (a type of fat) in the blood, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels, by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that produce triglycerides.

Glycyrrhetic acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the arteries and vasculature, a common underlying factor in the development of high cholesterol.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to help reduce triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder containing 6-8% glycyrrhetic acid.
Mix the powdered root with 500mg of silica filler and 50mg of magnesium stearate in a small bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture, producing 1000 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.
Take two capsules three times daily 30 minutes before meals or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with high cholesterol because they are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The plant's leaves also contain triterpenoid saponins that inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol into the bloodstream, reducing overall cholesterol levels. Additionally, dandelion has been found to increase bile production, which helps remove excess cholesterol from the liver and gallbladder, further contributing to its ability to help manage high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Cholagogue effect: Dandelion root stimulates the production of bile, which helps to remove excess cholesterol from the liver and intestines, reducing its absorption into the bloodstream.
Lipid-lowering activity: The bioactive compounds in dandelion root have been shown to inhibit the production of cholesterol in the liver, leading to a reduction in LDL (bad) cholesterol levels.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion root is rich in antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of high cholesterol and cardiovascular disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps lower LDL cholesterol and triglycerides.
Seeds: Containing a high amount of fatty acids, particularly alpha-linolenic acid, which can help reduce cholesterol levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh dandelion roots from the wild or cultivate them in your garden for three years.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm and dry place or using a dehydrator at 50°c for six hours.
Chop the dried roots into smaller pieces and then grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for ten minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered root with two parts of cornstarch and store it in an airtight container for up to six months or freeze it for longer storage.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with high cholesterol because of its natural ability to inhibit the production of triglycerides in the liver, reducing overall cholesterol levels. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to decrease LDL (bad) cholesterol and increase HDL (good) cholesterol, improving overall lipid profiles. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to its beneficial effects on cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a key factor in cardiovascular disease and high cholesterol.

Cholesterol lowering: The bioactive compounds in ginger, like gingerone, have been shown to inhibit cholesterol synthesis, increase bile acid excretion, and improve lipid profiles, all of which can help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels.

Platelet aggregation inhibition: Ginger's ability to inhibit platelet aggregation can help prevent blood clots from forming, reducing the risk of cardiovascular events such as heart attacks and strokes associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of the ginger plant contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to help reduce triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels.
Roots: Ginger roots contain a bioactive compound called 6-gingerol, which has been found to inhibit the formation of fatty streaks in arteries, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of zingiber officinale variety weighing approximately 500 grams.
Wash the ginger roots thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the washed ginger roots in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 48 hours or until crisp.
Grind the dried ginger into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 200 grams.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the ground ginger powder and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Safflower capsules helps with high cholesterol because they contain a powerful compound called thomsonin, which has been shown to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. Additionally, safflower capsules contain a unique fatty acid profile that helps to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked to high cholesterol. By reducing inflammation and lowering LDL cholesterol, safflower capsules can help to improve overall cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of heart disease.
remedy_properties: Omega-6 fatty acids: Safflower oil, derived from the seeds of the safflower plant, contains a rich source of omega-6 fatty acids, specifically linoleic acid, which helps to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by reducing triglycerides and increasing HDL (good) cholesterol.

Antioxidants: Safflower capsules contain antioxidants like vitamin E, beta-carotene, and polyphenols, which help to protect the body against oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which are linked to high cholesterol and cardiovascular disease.

Statin-like compounds: Safflower oil contains statin-like compounds that inhibit the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, the enzyme responsible for producing cholesterol in the liver, thereby reducing overall cholesterol levels.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds contain alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), an omega-3 fatty acid that helps reduce triglycerides and LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels.
Roots: The roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat cardiovascular diseases, including high cholesterol, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest carthamus tinctorius flowers and dry them in a clean well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 30 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered carthamus tinctorius with 1000 milligrams of gelatin in a small bowl for five minutes.
Add a few drops of purified water to the mixture and stir until it forms a smooth paste that can be filled into capsules using a capsule machine for about 10 minutes.
Fill the capsules immediately after preparation and store them in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months or longer if stored properly.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for high cholesterol are natural supplements that combine the power of herbs to help lower and manage cholesterol levels. These capsules work by reducing LDL (bad) cholesterol, increasing HDL (good) cholesterol, and improving blood lipid profiles. Examples of herbal capsules that have been shown to be effective include those containing berberine, artichoke extract, and garlic powder. By using these supplements, individuals can improve their overall heart health, reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, and enjoy a better quality of life with improved energy levels and reduced symptoms of high cholesterol.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to high cholesterol that are treated with herbal capsules are cardiovascular conditions such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and atherosclerosis. Herbal supplements like garlic, gugulipid, and omega-3 fatty acids have been traditionally used to lower cholesterol levels, improve circulation, and reduce blood pressure. Other herbs like berberine, turmeric, and ginger also show potential benefits in managing cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving lipid profiles.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with high cholesterol is a blend of Artichoke leaf extract, Turmeric root powder, and Red yeast rice. Artichoke leaf extract supports liver function and bile production, while Turmeric root powder reduces inflammation and improves cardiovascular health. Red yeast rice, rich in lovastatin, helps to lower LDL cholesterol levels. This synergistic blend of herbs not only addresses the root causes of high cholesterol but also promotes overall cardiovascular well-being and reduces the risk of heart disease.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, herbal capsules, particularly those made from curcumin or turmeric, have shown significant benefits in reducing high cholesterol levels. The study found that curcumin and turmeric resulted in a reduction of total cholesterol (TC) by 25.13 mg/dl, low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C) by 39.83 mg/dl, and triglyceride (TG) by 33.65 mg/dl, while also increasing high-density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C) by 4.31 mg/dl. These findings suggest that herbal capsules containing curcumin or turmeric can be a useful adjunctive treatment for managing high cholesterol levels.
